source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane / Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Eugene Island Block 360

event.top_event: Mechanic hand injury from rotating fan belt contact
actions_taken: First aid administered to mechanic's left hand on platform
Mechanic evacuated by helicopter to shorebase
On-site investigation conducted by BSEE on 22 March 2021
Operating procedures and LOTO policy reviewed
event.summary: On 16 March 2021 at approximately 2030 hours, a mechanic suffered two fractured fingers on his left hand when he entered the compressor cooling fan housing to investigate a vibration alarm and placed his hand on a fan belt that was still rotating. The compressor had been shut down via E-stop relay, but residual energy in the fan belt was not properly isolated or dissipated. The incident was caused by human error (inattention to task) and failure to implement Stop Work Authority, Job Safety Analysis, and Lockout Tagout procedures.
recommendations: Implement Stop Work Authority prior to personnel entering confined spaces or equipment housings
Require Job Safety Analysis (JSA) completion before maintenance work on rotating equipment
Enforce Lockout Tagout (LOTO) procedures to isolate and dissipate all stored and residual energy
Ensure compliance with D-5 Energy Isolation LOTO Policy regarding relief and restraint of hazardous stored energy

threats.name: Failure to isolate residual energy in fan belt after compressor shutdown
Mechanic entering compressor housing without proper energy isolation procedures
Contact between mechanic's hand and rotating fan belt

consequences.name: Mechanic hand injury - two fractured fingers

controls.name: E-stop relay on compressor panel
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to stop compressor operation

supporting_text: The B-Operator arrived at the compressor panel and pulled the E-stop relay to stop all input sources to the panel.
The E-stop relay prevents the compressor from being put back in service until it is reset again at the panel.
The compressor shut-in due to vibration from the cooling fan.

controls.name: Lockout Tagout (LOTO) procedures
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure all stored and residual energy is relieved before maintenance

supporting_text: Lockout Tagout (LOTO procedures) should have been implemented on the compressor prior to entering the compressor cooling fan housing.

Following the application of lockout or tagout devices to energy isolating devices, all potentially hazardous stored or residual energy shall be relieved, disconnected, restrained, and otherwise rendered safe.

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to halt unsafe work
controls.lod_basis: Management control to prevent unauthorized or unsafe maintenance activities

supporting_text: The AI found that stop work authority should have been implemented prior to the mechanic entering the compressor cooling fan housing.
BSEE also found that the probable cause of the injury was a failure to implement Stop Work Authority prior to the Mechanic entering the compressor cooling fan housing.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to identify hazards and required controls before maintenance work

supporting_text: A Job Safety Analysis (JSA) should have been filled out prior to the Mechanic entering the compressor cooling fan housing to troubleshoot the vibration.
A JSA should have been implemented to allow the Mechanic to enter the compressor cooling fan housing.

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (gloves)
controls.lod_basis: PPE to reduce severity of hand injury from rotating equipment contact

supporting_text: The Mechanic stepped into the housing wearing mechanic brand gloves.
The Mechanic was wearing gloves at the time of the incident.

controls.name: Compressor vibration alarm system
controls.lod_basis: Automated detection system to alert operators to equipment issues

supporting_text: On 16 March 2021, at approximately 2000 hours, an alarm sounded as the platform and its gas compressor shut in.
It was determined from the Compressor's Pneumatic Components Panel, that the compressor shut-in due to vibration from the cooling fan.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
